blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Negative refresh paths behaved clearly for static and missing accounts, but the required refresh-success path could not be exercised within the isolated fixture because refresh-codex is hardwired to the live OpenAI OAuth refresh endpoint.
A proactive refresh attempt was observable before forwarding, but the success path could not be validated because the runtime cannot be redirected from the user surface to a local OAuth token stub.
The 401-triggered refresh path is visible, but the success retry case cannot be validated locally because the runtime cannot point refresh traffic at a local OAuth token stub from the user surface.

appliedUpdates.description: Added flow validator guidance for shell, curl, and tuistory surfaces plus isolated fixture/evidence boundaries for terminal-operator-console validation.

Documented discovered validation quirks: hardwired OAuth refresh paths block local success-path refresh testing, tuistory PTYs need explicit close, and runtime-down selected refresh still surfaces remote OAuth failure text.
